schema.appDescription: Calculate gratuity amount for private sector employees under the Payment of Gratuity Act 1972. Know exact gratuity, tax-free limit, and taxable portion.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Gratuity Calculator. Smart defaults are pre-filled.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and find the best option.

faq.q: What is the gratuity formula in India?
faq.a: Gratuity = (Basic + DA) × 15 ÷ 26 × Years of Service. The '15' is for 15 days of salary, '26' converts monthly salary to daily (26 working days/month). Example: ₹50,000 basic, 10 years = 50,000 × 15 ÷ 26 × 10 = ₹2,88,462.

Gratuity up to ₹20 lakh (revised limit) is tax-free for private sector employees under the Payment of Gratuity Act. Government employees get full tax exemption. Amounts above ₹20 lakh are taxable as income from salary.

faq.q: When am I eligible for gratuity?
faq.a: Gratuity is payable on: resignation/retirement after 5+ years continuous service, termination (no minimum service requirement), death or disability (no minimum service). The 5-year rule is waived in case of death. Gratuity must be paid within 30 days of becoming payable.

examples.heading: ₹50,000 basic, 10 years service
examples.text: Gratuity = 50,000 × 15/26 × 10 = ₹2,88,462. Fully tax-free (below ₹20 lakh limit). That's ₹28,846/year of service. For 20 years, gratuity = ₹5,76,923 — still tax-free as under ₹20 lakh.

examples.heading: High earner — ₹2 lakh basic, 15 years
examples.text: Gratuity = 2,00,000 × 15/26 × 15 = ₹17,30,769. Tax-free. For 20 years: ₹23,07,692 — ₹3,07,692 above ₹20 lakh limit is taxable at slab rate.
